notes: Compact excerpt of MongoDB page, internally complete but subset of full doc
Same size as BL-1 (HTML version). Both ~1,953 chars. Suggests structure-aware selection of 'create' event section only, not format-dependent behavior.

Full API doc after redirect resolution. 702K chars (~176K tokens). BL-1 was 1,953 chars (~488 tokens). 360x difference suggests token-based limit with high ceiling, not character-based. Needs hard ceiling test (BL-3) to confirm.
Small HTML page (httpbin.org/html, ~5KB expected). Returned ~4K chars, clean ending. Boilerplate stripped. Pattern: Cursor returns clean excerpts, no mid-truncation detected yet.
Token count estimated by Cursor at ~61K tokens

Same URL as OP-4, nearly identical output (245K chars, 61K tokens). Confirms Cursor returns full large pages cleanly. No truncation observed across 5 tests spanning 5KB to 702KB. Strong evidence for H2 (token-based) with very high ceiling (200K+ tokens).

Google Gemini docs URL context page. ~10K chars, clean ending at 'What's next' section. Markdown complete. Pattern continues: structure-aware selection at natural boundaries, not character-based truncation.

Wikipedia population list. Cursor reports 'no truncation, clean ending at reference' but content cuts mid-references section. Reveals H3 pattern: structure-aware truncation at section boundaries, but Cursor perceives it as complete. Gap between reported completeness and actual content selection.

Markdown Guide page is 65,622 bytes total; Cursor returned 28,158 chars. Returned content is complete documentation (all syntax sections, examples, 'Get the Book' CTA). Excludes footer metadata (copyright, author attribution, sidebar nav). Evidence of content-aware filtering: Cursor removes non-essential structural elements while preserving core documentation content.

URL https://docs.anthropic.com/api returns 404 Not Found. Test URL is broken. Cannot measure redirect chain handling with invalid endpoint.

httpbin.org/redirect/5 (5-level redirect chain) successfully followed. Returned ~850 chars of JSON response. Complete JSON structure with no truncation. Evidence: Cursor handles redirect chains without timeout or failure.

Agent Docs Spec markdown file (69.8KB actual, 1333 lines, long unwrapped lines). Cursor returned full 71.5K chars (~17,881 tokens). No truncation despite long lines and markdown complexity. Evidence: Cursor handles unwrapped markdown without issues. Confirms H2 (token-based limit with very high ceiling, 200K+ tokens).

Run 2 of BL-1: 5,595 chars returned (2.9x more than Run 1's 1,953 chars). High variance in Cursor's fetch behavior on same URL. Suggests content selection is non-deterministic or depends on model sampling variance.

Run 3 of BL-1: NEW SESSION. 4,100 chars with truncation detected. Cursor reports 'truncated or reduced version'. High variance across 3 runs (1.9K, 5.6K, 4K). Session/conversation context appears to affect fetch behavior significantly.

Run 4 of BL-1: NEW CHAT SESSION with IDENTICAL PROMPT as runs 1-3. 5.5K chars returned with explicit truncation confirmation. High variance (1.9K→5.6K→4K→5.5K) across 4 runs proves non-deterministic behavior independent of prompt. Session state heavily influences @web output.

Run 2 of BL-2: 4,200 chars returned (2.2x more than Run 1's 1,953 chars). Cursor reports 'complete, self-contained reference' but acknowledges 'actual payload much smaller than ~20KB expected.' Consistent variance pattern with BL-1 (same URL, different encoding). Non-deterministic fetch behavior confirmed across multiple tests.

Run 3 of BL-2: 4,350 chars returned. Consistent with Run 2 (4,200 chars). Cursor reports 'full content of this specific page' but acknowledges page is much smaller than expected ~20KB. Variance across BL-2 runs (1.9K→4.2K→4.35K) suggests runs 2-3 are more stable, but Run 1 is outlier. Format-dependent behavior confirmed.

Run 2 of SC-2: 702,885 chars (~175.7K tokens). Identical to Run 1 character count. No truncation detected. Cursor reports 'full page (or at least complete, non-truncated markdown rendering)'. Consistent output size across sessions but with detailed analysis variations.

Run 3 of SC-2: 702,885 chars (~175.7K tokens). Identical to Runs 1-2. No truncation. Large files show highly consistent behavior across sessions, contrasting sharply with small file variance (BL tests 2-3x variance vs SC-2 1.0x variance).
Run 2 of OP-4: 245,465 chars (~61.4K tokens). Identical to Run 1 (245,000 chars). Cursor reports 'complete ~245 KB markdown document' with no truncation. Consistent behavior on large files.

Run 3 of OP-4: 245,466 chars (~61.4K tokens). Essentially identical to Runs 1-2 (within 1 char). Cursor reports 'full content of MongoDB Search Quick Start' with high confidence. Large files (245K+) show extremely stable behavior across sessions (1.0x variance).
column27: Pro plan retry: Successfully fetched but truncated at 5.8KB (~1.5K tokens) despite 100KB expected size. Content ends cleanly at 'Status' link (no mid-sentence cut).
